title: Aristeguietia pseudarborea
intro: Aristeguietia pseudarborea, also known as False Forest Balsam, is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae, native to South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which may be beneficial in treating wounds, infections, and other inflammatory conditions. Bioactive constituents of Aristeguietia pseudarborea include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and respiratory issues.

It belongs to the genus Aristeguietia, which consists of various species of shrubs and small trees. The plant is native to South America and is closely related to other members of the tribe Eupatorieae. Aristeguietia pseudarborea is a relatively rare species and is often found in specific habitats within its native range.
